assumptions: The developer has no Zendesk Support account or subdomain.
The developer has a work email, company context, phone number, and a supported browser but no CLI, SDK, OAuth client, API token, or application code.

The developer does not have an administrator who can prepare an existing organization account; the new trial owner is expected to receive admin access, but the account form does not document the provisioning transition.

developer_goal: Create a Zendesk Support development trial, enter Agent Workspace as an admin or agent, execute the promoted browser-console Ticketing API quickstart, and verify the three generated tickets.

first_success.outcome: Use the JavaScript console inside a signed-in Zendesk Agent Workspace to execute the official loop of three Create Ticket API POST requests and verify the resulting Test ticket 1, Test ticket 2, and Test ticket 3 records.
first_success.observable_signal: The browser console lists three returned ticket objects; after reloading Agent Workspace and opening Views > Recently updated tickets, the three new test tickets appear.

route_selection.surface: Sunshine Platform: 10-minute Ticketing API quick start
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Sunshine Platform: 10-minute Ticketing API quick start
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Using the Ticketing API
candidates.reason: This longer Python and OAuth tutorial reads groups, updates a ticket, and creates a ticket; the documentation home explicitly promotes the 10-minute browser-console route as Create your first ticket.

candidates.name: Zendesk Apps Framework quickstart
candidates.reason: The documentation home presents this as a separate 15-minute app-framework path after the general developer-account and first-ticket route.

candidates.name: Messaging SDK, Help Center, Voice, Custom Data, Sales CRM, webhooks, and integration paths
candidates.reason: These are equally real product surfaces, but the documentation home explicitly labels the Ticketing API route Create your first ticket and places it directly after developer-account creation.

required_path.action: Complete Zendesk account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Zendesk account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Zendesk candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The browser console lists three returned ticket objects; after reloading Agent Workspace and opening Views > Recently updated tickets, the three new test tickets appear.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A Zendesk Support account with agent or administrator access; Zendesk offers a free 14-day trial for testing.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2', 'S3']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'For the current nine-step trial form: work email, first name, last name, phone number, company country, company name, job title, company employee-count band, chosen Zendesk subdomain, and password.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S4']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'Administrator authority is required to activate first-time API access settings; the quickstart itself permits an admin or agent role for the browser-console request.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S5']}

external_gates.description: The documentation-home path requires a work-email-based 14-day Zendesk Support trial when no suitable instance exists.
The trial form collects personal and company data across nine steps and submits under Zendesk's Privacy Notice; marketing consent is separately presented.
The developer must select an available Zendesk subdomain that becomes the instance origin.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

excluded.item: Deleting the three test tickets from the list after experimentation; this is documented cleanup after the successful creation and verification boundary.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Trying other requests, the API reference, Postman, curl, API clients, common Ticketing API tasks, and community support.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Building a production application with OAuth, rotating or migrating credentials, pagination, rate-limit handling, security controls, error handling, observability, and data governance.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Apps Framework, Marketplace submission, Messaging SDKs, Help Center, Voice, Sales CRM, Live Chat, Custom Data, Profiles, Events, webhooks, and integration services.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Important update: Password access for APIs will soon be removed
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Important update: Password access for APIs will soon be removed

uncertainties.question: What replaces the quickstart's API password access prerequisite for a new July 2026 trial?
uncertainties.impact: The stated prerequisite is unavailable to new team-member accounts, so a literal reader cannot establish whether to activate API terms only, enable API tokens, create OAuth, enable end-user password access, or do nothing beyond the signed-in browser session.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The quickstart still requires and links password access, while current help says team-member password access is deprecated and unsupported for new accounts. The script supplies no explicit credentials and runs same-origin inside Agent Workspace.

uncertainties.question: What exact post-submit actions occur before a new trial owner reaches Agent Workspace?
uncertainties.impact: Email confirmation, anti-abuse review, launch setup, product selection, data-region choice, or guided onboarding could add pages and waits before the quickstart.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The registration page exposes nine input stages but the developer account guide only links to the live form and does not document provisioning or the first signed-in transition.

uncertainties.question: Does the advertised 10-minute duration include any account or API-access preparation?
uncertainties.impact: A new developer's actual time to first ticket can be much longer than the homepage label.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The quickstart label is 10 minutes, but its What you'll need section treats account, role, API access, and browser as pre-existing prerequisites.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Zendesk show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
